tools.name: search_tutors
tools.description: Search iTutorOnline's verified tutor directory. Filter by subject/keyword, country, spoken language, max hourly price (EUR), and minimum rating. Returns ranked tutor cards with rates, ratings, and profile links.

tools.name: get_tutor
tools.description: Get the full public profile for one tutor by their user id (the id shown in search results or in a /tutor_profile/<id> URL): headline, description, subjects, academic levels, rate, rating, credentials, languages, and how to book.

tools.name: get_tutor_reviews
tools.description: Get recent written reviews and the average score for a tutor, by their user id.

tools.name: list_subjects
tools.description: List the subjects tutors teach, with the search terms and synonyms that match each. Use before search_tutors to pick a good subject term.

tools.name: get_platform_info
tools.description: Explain how iTutorOnline works: pricing, the free first 30 minutes, hour packages, languages of instruction, how booking works, and the live count of verified tutors.

tools.name: prepare_booking
tools.description: Assemble a real tutoring booking for a parent/student to finish in one tap. Give the tutor id, how long, who it is for, and optionally a preferred time. Returns a price estimate, a booking brief, and the link the human opens to confirm and pay. Creates nothing and charges nothing itself.

tools.name: request_human_tutor
tools.description: Hand a tutoring request to the iTutorOnline team so they can line up a human tutor. Provide the subject and any details; optionally a contact email. Also returns tutors the student can book immediately.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_tutor" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the full public profile for one tutor by their user id (the id shown in search results or in a /tutor_profile/<id> URL): headline, description, subjects, ac…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_tutor, get_tutor_reviews · Private data: get_tutor, request_human_tutor · Public sink: request_human_tutor.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_tutor, get_tutor_reviews · Private data: get_tutor, request_human_tutor · Public sink: request_human_tutor

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
